<summary>

  <investigated>Git history was examined (commits 726ca4d, c5288d6, a9cf326 were upstream changes). The actual hook implementation was found in uncommitted working directory changes. Code review compared implementation against "Plan A" specification in docs/post-tool-hooks-proposal.html section 6. Files examined: src/event-log.ts (EventType enum), src/sessionManager.ts (tool_call_update event handlers), and actual events.jsonl output data.</investigated>
  <learned>The hook implementation is structurally correct - EventType was extended with "tool_call", logEvent() calls are properly placed in completed/failed branches, and type signatures pass tsc. However, a functional bug was discovered: the code logs tool call IDs (unique strings like "tooluse_LLOSqz9vVeE9QcOQw2fP4F") instead of tool names ("Read", "Bash", etc.). This happens because tool_call_update events contain up.toolCallId but not up.title - only the initial tool_call event has the title. This defeats the proposal's goal of creating tool usage heatmaps and aggregated statistics since every ID is unique and cannot be grouped by tool name. Additionally, no code currently reads these tool_call events, making it write-only data with no consumer.</learned>

  <notes>The implementation is type-safe and runs without crashes, but the logged data structure makes the feature's intended analytics impossible. The fix is straightforward (add Map lookup) and would also improve the existing toolLog display which currently shows cryptic IDs.</notes>
</summary>

<observation>
<type>bugfix</type>
<title>Added Tool Title Tracking to Fix Hook Data Bug (Part 1)</title>
<subtitle>Implemented _toolTitles Map in ChatSession to capture tool name at start event for later lookup.</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>Added _toolTitles optional Map field to ChatSession interface in sessionManager.ts to store toolCallId-to-title mappings</fact>
<fact>Modified tool_call event handler to extract toolCallId and store it alongside the title in the _toolTitles Map when tool execution starts</fact>
<fact>The Map is lazily initialized using nullish coalescing assignment operator when first tool call occurs</fact>
<fact>This fixes the first half of the bug where tool_call_update events were logging unique IDs instead of reusable tool names</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>The primary session implemented the first part of the fix for the PostToolUse hook bug. The issue was that tool_call_update events (completed/failed) only have access to up.toolCallId (a unique string like "tooluse_LLOSqz9...") but not the human-readable tool name (like "Read", "Bash"). To solve this, a _toolTitles Map was added to the ChatSession interface to track the toolCallId-to-title relationship. When a tool_call event fires (which has both the ID and title), the code now stores this mapping in the Map. The next step will be to modify the tool_call_update handlers (completed/failed branches) to look up the real tool name from this Map instead of logging the opaque ID. This will enable the intended analytics features like tool usage heatmaps and statistics that were specified in the original proposal.</narrative>

The primary session just completed the second part of the fix. They modified the tool_call_update handler to:

1. Extract the toolCallId from the update event
2. Look up the human-readable tool name from the _toolTitles Map that was populated during tool_call start
3. Use that resolved tool name in both the toolLog display and the logEvent call
4. Clean up the Map entry after the tool completes or fails to prevent memory leaks

This completes the bugfix - now the tool_call events logged to events.jsonl will contain actual tool names like "Read", "Bash", "Edit" instead of opaque IDs like "tooluse_LLOSqz9vVeE9QcOQw2fP4F".

The fix also improves the toolLog display (the ✅/❌ lines shown to users), which previously showed the cryptic IDs.

<narrative>The primary session completed the PostToolUse hook bugfix by modifying the tool_call_update event handlers in sessionManager.ts. Previously, these handlers logged up.toolCallId (unique strings like "tooluse_LLOSqz9vVeE9QcOQw2fP4F") instead of human-readable tool names. The fix implements a lookup pattern: when a tool_call_update arrives (completed or failed status), the code extracts the toolCallId, looks it up in the _toolTitles Map that was populated at tool_call start, and uses the resolved name in both the logEvent call and the toolLog display. This means events.jsonl will now contain analyzable data like {"name":"Read","status":"completed"} instead of meaningless unique IDs, enabling the tool usage statistics and heatmaps that were the original goal of the hook proposal. The code also cleans up Map entries after use to prevent memory accumulation. The fix also improves the user experience since the toolLog (streamed to Telegram) now shows "✅ Read" instead of "✅ tooluse_LLOSqz9...".</narrative>
